skill.name: research
skill.description: Coordinates multi-source research across web, local documentation, and code references with confidence-based synthesis.
skill.summary: Run structured research workflows that compare web, docs, and code findings.
skill.author: Consiliency

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many Ruby backtick and weak-crypto indicators, but reviewed locations are Markdown fences, inline paths, diagrams, model names, and documentation text rather than executable code. No prompt injection, malicious command execution, or credential exfiltration evidence was found; the real residual risk is intended use of WebSearch plus local Read/Grep operations.

low_findings.title: Intended Web and Local File Research Access
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to use WebSearch and local Read, Glob, and Grep tools for research. This is consistent with the skill purpose, but users should understand that prompts may cause searches of web sources, local documentation, and codebase files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced documentation explicitly lists WebSearch and local Read, Glob, and Grep tools. This is a legitimate capability rather than malicious behavior, but it creates a clear data-access consideration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences, output templates, diagrams, inline file paths, and example command syntax. No executable Ruby code, shell invocation, or user-controlled command construction was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files are Markdown documentation and the flagged characters appear inside fenced examples or inline formatting. There is no script file or executable command path at these locations.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Findings Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography alerts at the cited lines do not show cryptographic algorithms, hash construction, or security-sensitive code. The reviewed text is skill metadata or documentation paths, so the alerts are false positives.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain a plain description and an ai-docs guides path. They do not contain MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any code that performs cryptographic operations.

content.value_statement: Research tasks often miss context when they use only one source type. This skill organizes parallel web, documentation, and code research, then synthesizes findings with confidence levels.
actual_capabilities: Defines quick, standard, and extensive research modes with different depth and timeout expectations.
Combines WebSearch, local documentation search, and codebase search into one research workflow.
Provides output formats for concise answers, standard reports, and comprehensive research reports.
Uses confidence levels to show how strongly multiple sources support each finding.
Documents timeout handling so partial results can still be synthesized.
limitations: Web research requires network access and may be limited by rate limits or unavailable pages.
Local documentation and code research depend on files that exist in the active workspace.
Quick mode may miss nuance because it uses fewer sources and less cross-validation.
The skill provides research process guidance, not a guaranteed truth source.

use_cases.title: Validate a Technical Decision
use_cases.description: Compare web documentation, local implementation examples, and internal notes before choosing a library or architecture pattern.

use_cases.title: Answer Product Research Questions
use_cases.description: Gather current information and summarize confidence levels for market, workflow, or tool comparison questions.

use_cases.title: Investigate Codebase Patterns
use_cases.description: Find local examples and related documentation before implementing a feature or changing an existing workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Fact Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a fast answer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use quick mode to research: [topic]. Return a concise answer, sources, and confidence level.

prompt_templates.title: Standard Technical Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Use standard mode to research: [technical question]. Compare web sources, local docs, and code examples where available.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Options
prompt_templates.prompt: Use standard mode to compare [option A] and [option B] for [context]. Include tradeoffs, evidence, and confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Research Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced research for planning or documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use extensive mode to research [topic]. Include executive summary, key findings, conflicts, implementation recommendations, and source confidence.

output_examples.input: Use quick mode to research whether a framework supports server-side rendering.
output_examples.output: A short answer with two sources, a confidence label, and a note about any missing or conflicting evidence.

output_examples.input: Use standard mode to research WebSocket authentication patterns for this repository.
output_examples.output: A structured report with summary, key findings, implementation notes, sources, and confidence per finding.

output_examples.input: Use extensive mode to compare two agentic coding tools for a team workflow.
output_examples.output: A comprehensive report with comparison analysis, recommendations, conflicting information, and follow-up research areas.

best_practices: Choose quick mode for simple fact checks and standard mode for most technical questions.
Use extensive mode only when cross-validation, comparisons, or architecture decisions justify the extra time.
Review low-confidence and conflicting findings before making important decisions.
anti_patterns: Do not use quick mode for complex architecture decisions or policy-sensitive research.
Do not treat a single web result as verified when the report marks confidence as low.
Do not include secrets or private credentials in research prompts.

faq.question: What does this skill research?
faq.answer: It researches user questions using web sources, local documentation, and codebase examples when those tools are available.

faq.question: Which mode should I use first?
faq.answer: Use standard mode for most tasks. Use quick mode for simple checks and extensive mode for high-impact decisions.

faq.question: Does it access the internet?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented web researcher uses WebSearch for current information when network access is available.

faq.question: Can it inspect my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented code researcher uses Read and Grep to find local implementation examples.

faq.question: How does it handle conflicting information?
faq.answer: It calls out conflicting findings and uses confidence levels to show how strongly sources agree.

faq.question: Is this safe to publish?
faq.answer: The audit found no malicious code. Users should still treat web and local file access as intentional research capabilities.
